content.problemStatement:
Chef's coding class is very famous in Chefland.

This year XXX students joined his class and each student will require one chair to sit on. Chef already has YYY chairs in his class. Determine the minimum number of new chairs Chef must buy so that every student is able to get one chair to sit on.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains two integers XXX and YYY — the number of students in the class and the number of chairs Chef already has.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum number of extra chairs Chef must buy so that every student gets one chair.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y — the number of students and the number of chairs already available.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer on its own line: the minimum number of additional chairs Chef must buy.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: 20 students, 14 chairs → need 6 more.
Test case 2: 41 students, 41 chairs → need 0.
Test case 3: 35 students, 0 chairs → need 35.
Test case 4: 50 students, 100 chairs → need 0.
Case 1: no students, no chairs → 0.\nCase 2: 5 students, 10 chairs → already enough → 0.\nCase 3: 7 students, 3 chairs → need 4 more.
1000 students and no chairs, so Chef must buy 1000 chairs.

hidden.label: edge case where chairs exceed students

solution.approach: For each test case compute diff = X - Y. If diff is positive, Chef needs diff more chairs; otherwise he needs none. Output max(0, diff). This runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time computation per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
